diagnosis_text: Pod could not be scheduled because its memory request exceeds the allocatable memory available on any node in the cluster.
fix_plan_text:
1. Inspect the pod scheduling events to confirm an insufficient memory condition.
2. Reduce the memory requests/limits to realistic values or provision nodes with higher memory capacity.
3. Re-apply the workload and confirm it schedules successfully.
actions_text:
kubectl -n infra-lab-4mkeig describe pod module-w-ja9f-8d5988b97-rsvjm
kubectl -n infra-lab-4mkeig get events --sort-by=.lastTimestamp
kubectl -n infra-lab-4mkeig get deployment module-w-ja9f -o yaml
# Edit resources.requests.memory / resources.limits.memory to fit cluster capacity
kubectl -n infra-lab-4mkeig rollout restart deployment/module-w-ja9f
verification_text:
1. `kubectl -n infra-lab-4mkeig get pods` shows the pod scheduled and progressing to Running.
2. `kubectl -n infra-lab-4mkeig get events` no longer reports FailedScheduling due to insufficient memory.
rollback_text: If the new resource settings cause instability, revert to the previous workload manifest and restore the last known good deployment configuration.

diagnosis_text: Pod could not be scheduled because the workload nodeSelector does not match any node labels in the cluster.
fix_plan_text:
1. Inspect scheduling events to confirm a node selector mismatch.
2. Compare the workload nodeSelector with actual node labels.
3. Update the nodeSelector to valid labels or remove it if unnecessary.
4. Re-apply the workload and verify successful scheduling.
actions_text:
kubectl -n orders-prod-da9h4h describe pod api-w-hz4y-0
kubectl get nodes --show-labels
kubectl -n orders-prod-da9h4h get statefulset api-w-hz4y -o yaml
# Edit spec.template.spec.nodeSelector to match an existing node label
kubectl -n orders-prod-da9h4h rollout restart statefulset/api-w-hz4y
verification_text:
1. `kubectl -n orders-prod-da9h4h get pods` shows the pod scheduled onto a node.
2. `kubectl -n orders-prod-da9h4h get events` no longer reports FailedScheduling due to node selector mismatch.
rollback_text: If the updated node selector routes workloads to the wrong nodes, revert the selector to the previous value and redeploy the workload.

diagnosis_text: Pod could not be scheduled because the workload nodeSelector does not match any node labels in the cluster.
fix_plan_text:
1. Inspect scheduling events to confirm a node selector mismatch.
2. Compare the workload nodeSelector with actual node labels.
3. Update the nodeSelector to valid labels or remove it if unnecessary.
4. Re-apply the workload and verify successful scheduling.
actions_text:
kubectl -n orders-prod-2wzwuj describe pod module-w-2g5c-0
kubectl get nodes --show-labels
kubectl -n orders-prod-2wzwuj get statefulset module-w-2g5c -o yaml
# Edit spec.template.spec.nodeSelector to match an existing node label
kubectl -n orders-prod-2wzwuj rollout restart statefulset/module-w-2g5c
verification_text:
1. `kubectl -n orders-prod-2wzwuj get pods` shows the pod scheduled onto a node.
2. `kubectl -n orders-prod-2wzwuj get events` no longer reports FailedScheduling due to node selector mismatch.
rollback_text: If the updated node selector routes workloads to the wrong nodes, revert the selector to the previous value and redeploy the workload.
